{"key_event":"Great Leap communes and Leninist party-state consolidation","reasoning":"By 1959 the CCP had built a pervasive Leninist party-state through land reform, campaigns, collectivization, danwei, communes, planning, and coercive surveillance, giving it overwhelming reach despite administrative distortions. Autonomous civil society was reduced to a very low level as landlords, private business, religious bodies, independent intellectuals, clan authorities, and peasant/family autonomy were destroyed, absorbed, or intimidated into state-led structures.","society_change":-1.7,"society_power":1.5,"state_change":2.3,"state_power":8.8}
